subject: ENGLISH
title: Digraph Detectives
objective: Recognize taught ch and sh digraphs.
introduction: In the taught words, ch or sh uses two letters to represent one main sound. English has exceptions, so use the lesson examples. You will explore examples, sort cards, practice, and answer five questions.

vocabulary.definition: A key word used in the lesson “Digraph Detectives”.
A key word used in the lesson “Digraph Detectives”.

activities.instruction: Study the idea and listen to the narration.
activities.content: In the taught words, ch or sh uses two letters to represent one main sound. English has exceptions, so use the lesson examples.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: Text alternative: In the taught words, ch or sh uses two letters to represent one main sound. English has exceptions, so use the lesson examples.

activities.instruction: Explore each learning token, then read the examples.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: A numbered list of the same examples is available for keyboard and screen-reader use.

activities.instruction: Sort each card into “fits the lesson” or “does not fit.”

feedback.correct: Your groups follow the lesson rule.
feedback.retry: Use the explanation, not color or position.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Tap an item and then tap a labeled category; dragging is optional.

feedback.retry: Read the lesson rule again. chair and stop do not fit.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: All choices are visible text and selectable with TalkBack.

activities.instruction: Match each example to the lesson idea.

feedback.retry: Return to the explanation and compare one pair at a time.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: A list-based matching control replaces drag gestures.

activities.instruction: Complete the guided review in order.

feedback.correct: You are ready for the five-question check.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Up and down buttons provide an alternative to dragging.

options.text: In the taught words, ch or sh uses two letters to represent one main sound. English has exceptions, so use the lesson examples.

items.explanation: The correct response is “In the taught words, ch or sh uses two letters to represent one main sound. English has exceptions, so use the lesson examples.” because it follows the lesson explanation: In the taught words, ch or sh uses two letters to represent one main sound. English has exceptions, so use the lesson examples.

The correct response is “chair” because it follows the lesson explanation: In the taught words, ch or sh uses two letters to represent one main sound. English has exceptions, so use the lesson examples.

items.prompt: Which choice does not follow the lesson idea?
items.explanation: The correct response is “stop” because it follows the lesson explanation: In the taught words, ch or sh uses two letters to represent one main sound. English has exceptions, so use the lesson examples.

The correct response is “ship” because it follows the lesson explanation: In the taught words, ch or sh uses two letters to represent one main sound. English has exceptions, so use the lesson examples.

items.prompt: Which example should be used in the final review?
items.explanation: The correct response is “ship” because it follows the lesson explanation: In the taught words, ch or sh uses two letters to represent one main sound. English has exceptions, so use the lesson examples.

assetSpecs.prompt: Friendly Grade 3 cartoon storybook concept board for Digraph Detectives; rounded shapes, warm dark-brown outlines, tropical palette, no embedded text, uncluttered small-screen layout. Compose overlays provide labels and interactive targets.
assetSpecs.contentDescription: Five connected learning tokens for exploring examples in Digraph Detectives.
